title: Adab of Quran recitation
summary: Begin with sincerity, cleanliness, humility, and a steady pace. Tajweed is a means to honor Allah's words.
practice: Read one short surah slowly and avoid rushing the endings.

title: Articulation points
summary: Makharij are where letters exit from the mouth and throat. Learning them helps prevent major pronunciation mistakes.
practice: Practice throat letters: ء ه ع ح غ خ.

title: Qalqalah
summary: Qalqalah is a slight echo on ق ط ب ج د when they carry sukun or occur at a stop.

title: Ghunnah
summary: Ghunnah is a nasal sound commonly held for two counts on noon and meem with shaddah.
practice: Hold the nasal sound in إِنَّ and ثُمَّ.

title: Madd basics
summary: Madd means lengthening. Basic madd is usually held for two counts when a madd letter appears.
practice: Count gently while reading قَالَ and يَقُولُ.

title: Noon sakinah and tanween
summary: Noon sakinah and tanween rules include clear pronunciation, merging, changing, and hiding depending on the next letter.
practice: Mark examples in Juz Amma and listen to a qualified reciter.
